text:
Idea Generation with SCAMPER Prompts

Below are some prompts to help you generate design solution ideas. These are called SCAMPER prompts, they are a series of ‘what if’ questions that can help you think of new ideas by creatively changing your existing design solution sketches. SCAMPER is just one of many such design ideation tools. Take some time to read these prompts with your class to make sure you understand all of the prompts. Each prompt will help you build on the ideas you already generated in the introductory activity.

Then, you can choose one prompt and generate a design solution idea for your stakeholders using that prompt. Sketch your idea. Then explain your design solution idea in the text box below each sketch box. In the text box, you will write down what prompt you used and explain how your design solution works.

You will work silently during this activity. Take time to think of your ideas, sketch, and write. Try your best not to disturb the other students in the class so everyone can have quiet time to think.

Below are sketch boxes and text boxes for three sketches. (You also can use pencil and paper.) You can add additional sketch and text boxes for any additional ideas you generate.

Prompts

  • SCAMPER Prompts
  • Think of one of your design solution ideas from the introduction. Then build on that idea using one of the following prompts.
    • Substitute - what/who else instead? Substitute a different material, color, or part in your design.
    • Combine - what two or more parts, ideas, or functions can be combined in your design?
    • Adapt - what else is like this? Add additional parts or features to your design.
    • Modify, magnify, and minimize - change/made bigger or smaller to make your design easier to use. For example, the complexity of the design, unlimited or limited budget.
    • Put to others uses - new ways to use it?
    • Eliminate - what one part or feature from your design can be reduced/removed?
    • Rearrange - rearrange the parts or features of your design.
In a sentence or two, describe your design solution sketch. How does your idea work? What are its key features and details?
In a sentence or two, describe your design solution sketch. How does your idea work? What are its key features and details?
In a sentence or two, describe your design solution sketch. How does your idea work? What are its key features and details?

content.prompt:
This is a “Concept Generation, Drawing Ideas” activity of a front-end design process for middle and high school students, and students have generated ideas based on the following instructions
“Idea Generation with SCAMPER Prompts
Below are some prompts to help you generate design solution ideas. These are called SCAMPER prompts, they are a series of ‘what if’ questions that can help you think of new ideas by creatively changing your existing design solution sketches. SCAMPER is just one of many such design ideation tools. Take some time to read these prompts with your class to make sure you understand all of the prompts. Each prompt will help you build on the ideas you already generated in the introductory activity.
Then, you can choose one prompt and generate a design solution idea for your stakeholders using that prompt. Sketch your idea. Then explain your design solution idea in the text box below each sketch box. In the text box, you will write down what prompt you used and explain how your design solution works.
You will work silently during this activity. Take time to think of your ideas, sketch, and write. Try your best not to disturb the other students in the class so everyone can have quiet time to think.
Below are sketch boxes and text boxes for three sketches. (You also can use pencil and paper.) You can add additional sketch and text boxes for any additional ideas you generate.
Prompts
SCAMPER Prompts
Think of one of your design solution ideas from the introduction. Then build on that idea using one of the following prompts.
Substitute - what/who else instead? Substitute a different material, color, or part in your design.
Combine - what two or more parts, ideas, or functions can be combined in your design?
Adapt - what else is like this? Add additional parts or features to your design.
Modify, magnify, and minimize - change/made bigger or smaller to make your design easier to use. For example, the complexity of the design, unlimited or limited budget.
Put to others uses - new ways to use it?
Eliminate - what one part or feature from your design can be reduced/removed?
Rearrange - rearrange the parts or features of your design.” 
As a front-end design expert teacher,  give feedback to improve it. Your feedback should be based on user specifications or inference from the contents generated by users, and provide semantic stimuli from a set of advice or questions. Your feedback follows rules or algorithms that are adapted to semantic, conceptual, or temporal associations. Your feedback directly addresses students and should not be general. Your response should have a header in bold letters, "AI support for Idea Generation with SCAMPER Prompts."
